name: GeoSight
description: UNICEF's open source geospatial data platform, enhanced during the "Ahead of the Storm" hackathon to support disaster preparedness and child-centric climate risk analysis.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)
provenance.locator: Day 1 and 2; Hackathon – Building for UNICEF

name: Wikipedia
description: The free, collaboratively edited encyclopedia. The Edit-A-Thon expanded Wikipedia's coverage of global digital policy, adding more than 1,000 edits and 16,500 words across key articles.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)
provenance.locator: Day 1 and 2; Edit-A-Thon – Knowledge as a Commons

name: Matrix
description: An open standard and open source project for secure, decentralised real-time communication, stewarded by the Matrix.org Foundation and commercially supported by Element.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)
provenance.locator: Day 2 Breakout 1 (Amandine Le Pape, Element / Matrix.org Foundation)

name: Arduino
description: Open source electronics platform combining open hardware and software, referenced in the context of open source skills and education.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)

name: Drupal
description: Open source content management framework, referenced through Drupal/Palantir.Net and the Drupal Association across the DPI cross-border panel and Community Day.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)
provenance.locator: DPI Day; Global Cooperation for DPI panel (Tiffany Farriss)

name: Software Heritage Archive
description: An archive preserving the source code of more than 180 million open source projects, cited as essential shared infrastructure for digital cooperation.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)
provenance.locator: Day 3; Navigating Digital Cooperation panel (Roberto Di Cosmo)

name: Decidim
description: Open source participatory democracy platform, presented in the context of digital public goods as infrastructure.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)
provenance.locator: Community Day; Digital Public Goods as Infrastructure session

name: FIWARE
description: Open source platform and curated framework of components for building smart digital solutions, presented as a lesson source for open source DPI.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)
provenance.locator: Community Day; Open Standards, Open Source, Futures (FIWARE) session

name: Mojaloop
description: Open source software for interoperable digital payments and financial inclusion, stewarded by the Mojaloop Foundation.

provenance.source_title: UN Open Source Week 2025 Conference Report (RISE 2026:04)
provenance.locator: Community Day; Digital Public Goods as Infrastructure session (Paula Hunter, Mojaloop Foundation)
